title: cordon.checks.json
description: Per-repo configuration for cordon's checks engine. Most keys are a check id (omit one to use that check's defaults); `builtDirs` and `commands` configure the engine itself. Run `node checks/run.mjs --list` to see every check that applies here.

$schema.description: Reference to this schema for editor autocomplete and validation.

builtDirs.description: Candidate build-output dirs (first non-empty one wins). Drives the `built-dir` capability and the post-build invariants, so they agree on where the build is.

commands.description: This repo's own spawned specs (playwright, tsc, a bespoke audit) — merged with cordon's built-in invariants and run by the same engine. Spec code stays in the repo; this is just the inventory.

id.description: Stable id (verdict key, --only target). Unique across built-in checks too.

name.description: Human title for the verdict; defaults to id.

effect.description: Blast radius an agent risk-gates on (cordon's ladder). Required — an unclassified spec must never run as a safe read.

network.description: Set only when the spec itself reaches off-box.

interactive.description: Set only when the spec blocks on a TTY.

requires.description: Capabilities it needs (git/macos/ci/built-dir/<binary>, or !cap to negate); the engine skips it fail-soft when unmet.

phase.description: Where it runs around the build. Default pre-build.

timeout.description: Kill the spec after this many ms rather than wedge the gate.

exec.description: The process to spawn from the repo root.

env.description: Extra env, merged over the process env.

repository-policy.description: Repo hygiene: no tracked secrets/build output/conflict copies, unambiguous modules, pinned Actions. Universal rules need no config; the keys below tune the stack-specific ones.

forbiddenDirs.description: Directories whose tracked contents are always build output, never source.

conflictScanDirs.description: Extra trees to walk for untracked iCloud conflict copies ("name 2.ext"). Tracked copies are caught regardless; [] walks nothing.

checkNvmrc.description: When a .nvmrc is present, require the running Node major.minor to match it.

checkLockfile.description: When package.json and package-lock.json are both present, require their name/version/dependencies to agree.

allowTaggedActions.description: Accept tag/branch GitHub Action pins (e.g. @v5). Set false to enforce full commit-SHA pins (the hardened house policy).

idempotence.description: Run a build/verify command and assert it leaves the worktree byte-identical. Off until you set a command.

command.description: Shell command whose run must not change tracked or untracked files, e.g. "npm run build". null skips the check — set this to turn it on.

timeoutMs.description: Kill the command after this many milliseconds rather than wedge the gate.

internal-links.description: Internal link integrity over the built HTML. Universal rule; the two seams below adapt it to a site.

origin.description: Production origin (e.g. "https://example.com") to treat as same-site, so absolute canonical/og URLs are resolved against the build. null leaves external origins out of scope.

dynamicRoutePrefixes.description: Path prefixes served by functions/proxies, not emitted files — references under them are allowlisted instead of resolved on disk.

structural-html.description: Config for the "Structural HTML" check — no options.
